name: Glazed Irish Tea Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F with the rack in the center of the oven.
2. Generously grease a 9-inch (7-cup capacity) loaf pan and dust it with flour. Tap the pan over the sink to discard excess flour.
3. Cut parchment or waxed paper to fit the bottom of the pan and set aside.
4. Cream butter, sugar, and vanilla in a mixer until fluffy.
5. Add eggs one at a time, beating each until fluffy.
6. Add cream cheese and mix until well combined.
7. Sift together flour, baking powder, and salt.
8. In a small bowl, combine 1/4 cup of the sifted mixture with currants and stir to coat them evenly.
9. Gradually add remaining sifted mixture to the batter, alternating with buttermilk, mixing until smooth.
10. Stir in the remaining currants and flour until well combined.
11. Pour batter into the prepared pan and smooth the surface with a spatula.
12. Bake for about 1 hour and 25 minutes or until well-browned and a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean. The cake may crack on top.
13. Let the cake rest in the pan for 10 minutes before transferring it to a cooling rack using a flexible metal spatula.
14. Spread glaze over the warm cake once cooled, then let it cool completely.
15. Store the cake at room temperature in foil for up to 3 days or freeze airtight for up to 3 months.
